rulings.Case Title: MYRA DANIELS VS. ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP, et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 6, 2013, line 1. DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Moving party failed to shift burden. As to waiver and res judicata/collateral estoppel grounds, Defendant failed to show that all of Plaintiff's claims arise from incidents concerning backed-up sewage, which occurred anytime from April of 2004 through July 9, 2008 (date the Release was signed). The Court does not need to reach the issue of enforceability of the Release as none of the causes of action can be completely resolved at this point. As to the Statute of Limitations ground, Defendant's evidence does not support UMF 26 and does not satisfy Miller requirements for accrual of cause of action, i.e. (1) appreciable harm; and (2) awareness that defendant's negligence caused harm. Specifically, SAC 17-19 and Daniels Depo., Volume 1 at 21:16-21 does not satisfy "awareness" requirement. (Miller v. Lakeside Village Condominium Assn. (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 1611. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant failed to shift burden and establish that Defendant is entitled to summary adjudication as a matter of law due to plaintiff's failure to provide evidence to support punitive damages claim. Unlike in College Hospital, Plaintiff here alleged and provided evidence that defendant knew or possessed information regarding multiple floods, plaintiff's health issues and did not address the cause of the floods. Therefore, it is an issue of fact to be determined by the trier whether defendant's disregard of Plaintiff's rights for so contemptible that it would be looked down upon and despised by reasonable people. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: SHERENE RANDLE VS. WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. ALSO KNOWN AS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 6, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege all elements of each cause of action in light of judicially noticeable documents. Defendants' request for judicial notice is GRANTED. The Court does not make a ruling on preemption argument as it is premature without a valid cause of action. = (501/HMD)
